channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump backs Luna push for House parental proxy voting
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) has been scrambling to break an impasse over the push for proxy voting, after Luna joined with more than 200 Democrats and nine Republicans to use a procedure known as a discharge petition to circumvent leadership and force a vote on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Colleges say the Trump administration is using new tactics to expel international students
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A crackdown on foreign students is alarming colleges, who say the Trump administration is using new tactics and vague justifications to push some students out of the country. College officials worry the new approach will keep foreigners from wanting to study in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, ANNIE MA and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Living abroad and hungry to see a Chinese movie on the biggest of screens, some took action
item.description: COLUMBIA, Md. (AP) — A group of Chinese people decided they wanted the nation's biggest movie to be big in the United States, literally. So they made it really big. And therein lies a story.
item.dc:creator: FU TING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What to expect in South Korea now that Yoon has been removed from office
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The South Korean Constitutional Court’s decision to formally unseat President Yoon Suk Yeol is another test for the country’s democracy after the conservative-liberal divide deepened over his imposition of martial law and subsequent impeachment.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Storms kill 7 in the South and Midwest as forecasters warn of catastrophic rains, floods this week
item.description: LAKE CITY, Ark. (AP) — Standing alongside the twisted steel tractors on his family farm in northeast Arkansas on Thursday, Danny Qualls looked on while friends and relatives helped him begin cleaning up.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, GEORGE WALKER IV and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A look at Laura Loomer, longtime Trump ally criticized for racist posts and Sept. 11 conspiracies
item.description: Laura Loomer, a right-wing personality known for her incendiary social media presence, appeared to have been sidelined at points by Donald Trump's campaign and then by his administration. But she has long had the president's ear and may have it again, at least for now.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dad claims son was harassed by staff for wearing 'Let's Go Brandon' shirt, sues school district
item.description: A father has filed a lawsuit against an Ohio school district, claiming his son was punished for wearing a "Let's Go Brandon" T-shirt to school.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump sued over China tariffs
item.description: It marks the first known legal challenge against Trump’s tariffs, which have fulfilled a campaign promise and rattled financial markets.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the severe storms and flash flooding hitting parts of the US
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — At least seven people have been killed in a wide swath of violent storms and tornadoes that hit the South and Midwest, and officials are bracing for more severe weather and flooding in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A man has shot and killed a Catholic priest at his parish rectory in northeast Kansas
item.description: Church officials say a Catholic priest has been shot and killed in the northeastern Kansas town of Seneca.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dog saved after falling over 100 feet at Colorado National Monument
item.description: What started as a dog owner's worst nightmare ended in a heartwarming reunion thanks in part to a multi-agency effort by Colorado responders.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Kiser, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Suspect in fatal Tennessee hit-and-run shared victim's daughter's plea for answers on social media
item.description: Haley Layman, a fatal hit-and-run suspect in Tennessee, shared social media posts about the victim after allegedly striking him with her truck on March 31.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration fires staff of program that helps low-income households pay for heat
item.description: The Trump administration has laid off the entire staff of a $4.1 billion program that helps low-income households nationwide pay for heat.
item.dc:creator: Susan Haigh, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Drugged suspect in 'altered mental state' stabs six, along with himself, during DC rampage: police
item.description: An intoxicated person randomly stabbed six people and himself in Washington, D.C., according to the Metropolitan Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump approval slips to lowest point in second term: Survey
item.description: In the latest poll, 37 percent of respondents approved of Trump’s handling of the economy and 30 percent approved of his approach to addressing the high cost of living in the country.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tufts University declares support for student detained by ICE, seeks immediate release
item.description: The President of Tufts University filed a declaration in support of Rümeysa Öztürk, a student at the institution who is being detained by ICE. She was accused of supporting Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House postpones Saturday garden tours due to anti-Trump demonstrations
item.description: The White House is rescheduling one of its annual spring garden tour dates due to a "massive" anti-Trump demonstration expected to take place near 1600 Pennsylvania Ave.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KRON4

item.title: ICE releases man seeking life-saving transplant for brother after a month in custody
item.description: Jose Gregorio Gonzalez had been detained by ICE after he accompanied his brother, Jose Alfredo Gonzalez, to a kidney dialysis appointment.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tariffs hit tiny coral islands in the South Pacific and Arctic outposts
item.description: The Trump administration appears to be leaving no stone unturned with its sweeping tariffs around the globe, from rocky outcrops home to polar bears in the Arctic to tiny tropical islands.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dow drops 1,600 as US stocks lead worldwide sell-off after Trump’s tariffs cause a COVID-like shock
item.description: Financial markets around the world are reeling Thursday following President Donald Trump’s latest and most severe set of tariffs, and the U.S. stock market is taking the worst of it so far.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams will skip the Democratic primary and run for reelection as an independent
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams says he will pull out of the city’s Democratic primary and will instead run for reelection as an independent candidate.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Izaguirre, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's major tariff announcement
item.description: “Our country and its taxpayers have been ripped off for more than 50 years, but it’s not going to happen anymore,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, NewsNation

item.title: Outrage grows over Maryland man's mistaken deportation to El Salvador prison
item.description: In the 22 days since Maryland resident Kilmar Abrego Garcia was mistakenly deported to a notoriously violent prison in El Salvador, his young son has sought comfort in the scent of his missing father's clothes.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gravedigger arrested after baby's corpse found on West Virginia cemetery's grounds
item.description: A gravedigger at a Cabell County cemetery was arrested on Wednesday after a baby's corpse from 1982 and a busted casket were found at the Baylous Cemetery last week.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Taylor, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New documents describe physical condition of man allegedly held captive for decades in Connecticut
item.description: An investigator said the man's skin appeared "to be graying," and that his "physical condition resembles one who would be at a point at the end of their life.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, KRON4

item.title: Nintendo announces Switch 2 release date, new features in latest Direct showcase
item.description: After years of waiting, Nintendo fans finally have a release date for the Japanese company's new gaming console.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: NFL to use camera technology to measure first downs next season, approves more rule changes
item.description: The NFL announced a multitude of rule changes on Tuesday, including a new way to measure first downs for the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Jared Bush, Fox 5

item.title: Escaped otter is home safe but zoo says her pal is still on the loose
item.description: Ophelia the escaped zoo otter is back home but Louie remains elusive — perhaps in search of a mate. The two North American river otters escaped two weeks ago from the NEW Zoo & Adventure Park in Green Bay, Wisconsin. Ophelia was captured Friday night, the zoo said in a Facebook post.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: McMahon meeting with House Democrats over Education Department layoffs
item.description: Education Secretary Linda McMahon is having a meeting with House Democrats on Wednesday over their concerns surrounding the layoffs at the Department of Education, which she and President Trump have been vocal about trying to eliminate entirely. Rep. Mark Takano (Calif.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Jury selection begins in Karen Read's retrial over the death of her Boston police boyfriend
item.description: Jury selection began Tuesday in the retrial of Karen Read, less than a year after a judge declared a mistrial on charges that she was responsible for the death of her Boston police officer boyfriend.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Apparent explosion, fire injures about a dozen in Detroit apartment building
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — About a dozen people have been injured, including six children, following an apparent explosion and fire Monday morning at a 2-story apartment building in Detroit. The explosion was reported about 4 a.m., according to fire officials.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
